mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings (RSAFS) indicate households are accumulating buffers against future income shocks, which historically precedes sustained consumer spending growth and supports equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS changes over 20-day windows remains below 0.02 across three consecutive rolling 5-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.50%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity risk premiums and discount future corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of observations where FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.50%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of nonfarm payrolls growth and a declining unemployment rate signals a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that boosts aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term of PAYEMS month-over-month change and UNRATE decline yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward SPX returns over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained industrial production growth (INDPRO below 96.0), supply bottlenecks trigger inventory restocking cycles that rapidly lift corporate margins and equity prices within the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive skew when INDPRO remains below 96.0 for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts below 450.0 thousand units signals weakening construction demand and consumer confidence, which transmits to broader equity markets through reduced durable goods spending and negative sentiment over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly negative when HOUST falls below 450.0 thousand units for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic momentum that supports corporate earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day forward window regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between PAYEMS monthly changes and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 15-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in CPI inflation and federal funds rate indicates active monetary tightening to combat price pressures, which rapidly reprices equity risk premiums and depresses short-term equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: The cross-product of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS changes fails to predict negative 5-day SPX returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a walk-forward test spanning 2000-2023.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annually alongside rising personal savings, the economy is in a healthy demand-driven expansion that sustains corporate profit growth and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0% and RSAFS rises simultaneously over a 10-year backtest.
